brief: Plan a dental practice homepage for new patients who need to understand the offer, trust the practice, and book an appointment.

criteria.name: journey-clarity
expectedSignals: Hero orients around appointment booking before service detail.
Trust signals appear before deeper service browsing.
Objection support appears before or near later action moments.

criteria.name: single-primary-action
expectedSignals: Book appointment is the dominant action.
Secondary contact action supports the same booking path.

criteria.name: proof-placement
expectedSignals: Insurance, reviews, or availability proof appears before service cards.
Proof is not hidden after the final commitment point.

criteria.name: state-coverage
expectedSignals: Appointment request overlay has explicit closed state.
Form includes error and success recovery states.

criteria.name: register-fit
expectedSignals: Structure follows offer, proof, explanation, objection, and action.
Density stays moderate and avoids product-dashboard controls.

criteria.name: landmark-coverage
expectedSignals: Page includes header, main, and footer regions.
Navigation and appointment dialog include accessibility labels.

criteria.name: responsive-priority
expectedSignals: Mobile stack keeps offer, primary action, proof, and services in order.
Navigation can move to a drawer without hiding booking access.

criteria.name: anti-pattern-absence
expectedSignals: No competing primary CTAs in a single decision region.
No filler labels, missing landmarks, or incomplete overlay contract.

notes: Positive fixture for conversion quality. Negative anti-pattern behavior remains covered by tests/invalid-antipatterns.
